items.title: Why the U.S-Iran Memo of Understanding Is a Big Deal
items.summary: With the U.S. and Iran set to sign a memo of understanding Friday, we see Strait of Hormuz flows resuming relatively quickly and normalizing by mid- to late July. We see signs from both the U.S. and Iran that we think point to selling summer oil rallies. We offer further analysis herein.

items.title: European Equity Strategy: A Peace Deal's Broadening Potential
items.summary: Amid word of a U.S.-Iran interim deal, the STOXX 600 is trading at all-time highs, suggesting a meaningful portion of geopolitical risk premium has already been priced out. But we see a more uneven recovery beneath the surface, which creates scope for a second phase of the rebound.

items.title: Global FX Strategy: Fade EUR Rallies If Warsh Disappoints Hawks
items.summary: The Fed is unlikely to deliver the hawkish surprise needed to break the USD out of its 12-month range. While we've been more tactically cautious risk assets, a U.S.-Iran deal, Warsh delivering a neutral message and last week's successful major IPO could see risk stabilize and rally.

items.title: Metal Matters: Copper Book 2026
items.summary: We 're updating the structural investment case for copper, incorporating the growing importance of the energy transition, AI/datacenter demand, and evolving marginal cost dynamics driven by scrap. We conclude that copper has entered a structurally higher price regime, with details herein.

items.title: Global Commodities: Cocoa Looks Like a Key El Niño Play
items.summary: A strong El Niño now looks much more likely, with cocoa a key play among agricultural commodities as its production is highly concentrated in the areas most impacted. Consequently, we have revised our supply and demand balances and now expect the market to shift into a deficit.

items.title: North America Trucking: What’s Priced In? LTL Edition
items.summary: We follow our What’s Priced In? Truckload note with an examination of upside scenarios for LTLs. Based on this analysis, we downgrade ODFL to Sell and SAIA, KNX and CHRW to Neutral, as it becomes increasingly difficult to identify meaningful upside on fundamentals.

items.title: We're Upgrading Paychex to Buy
items.summary: We're upgrading Paychex to Buy, with a $140 target price. We expect organic revenue growth to accelerate in FY27 given a host of tailwinds and think a 4.73% dividend yield limits downside and remains attractive to investors, while valuation remains well below historical norms.

items.title: U.S. Semis: 800V DC Transition Drives Stronger Analog Recovery
items.summary: We explore the implications of the 800V DC transition for our analog and connector coverage and assess where we are in the analog cycle. We're most constructive on TXN, continue to favor MPWR, and initiate an upside Catalyst Watch on ON ahead of its September analyst day.

items.title: U.S. Rates Weekly: New Chair, Same Fed Inertia
items.summary: The Fed is likely to stay patient and balanced given recent benign inflation and labor data, and we continue to favor positive carry positions. Over the medium term, we still hold a long duration bias. We offer further analysis and strategies in this note.

items.title: We're Upgrading AMD to Buy
items.summary: We're upgrading AMD to Buy with a $575 target price. While we've been constructive on the inflection in agentic AI-led CPU demand, seeing AMD as the major beneficiary, we now also see it emerging as a legitimate second source in the GPU market. We offer further analysis in this note.

items.title: Global Macro Strategy: Buy the Flush, Watch the Fed
items.summary: Looking at equity-market re-entry strategies, we think VRP or lower POLLS work well — but if fundamentals change, investors need to react more quickly. Our Fed preview suggests the market pricing hikes six months out is on average wrong, but retracement in rates is somewhat short-lived.

items.title: PULSE Monitor: Supply Is Here
items.summary: The first Mega IPO is here, and supply has quietly returned with some recent equity issuance. The key from here is investor demand and the amount of available capital/portfolio space for new opportunities. An important starting point we explore is the lack of IPOs for much of this cycle.

items.title: Why We're Adding Continental to Our EU Focus List
items.summary: We provide a sum-of-the-parts sensitivity analysis for Continental as we enter a potentially newsflow-rich period with respect to the ContiTech sale process. We think returns to shareholders at our assumed valuation can still be substantial, and add Continental to our EU Focus List.

items.title: BMW Looks Very Oversold on China Panic, But Who Will Buy It?
items.summary: BMW is once again trading below net industrial cash, an extreme low valuation. Despite consistent and reasonable execution, BMW has reported falling EBIT margins, EPS, and free cash flow since a 2023 earnings peaks, raising the question of who would buy BMW given the current narrative.

items.title: KOSPI Upside Looks Intact ... But Be Selective
items.summary: We assess the scope of South Korea's equities rally. We raise our KOSPI target to 10,000 from 8,500, reflecting secular memory earnings growth as well as strong fiscal stimulus, but recommend a selective approach to equities, focusing on stocks with a high-quality earnings growth outlook.

items.title: How Low Can E&Ps Take Leverage?
items.summary: The U.S. E&P sector could be on the cusp of a generational deleveraging cycle, driven by a structurally re-rated commodity curve, sustained capital discipline and operational efficiency gains. We explore this thesis in more depth herein, note key risks, and review our preferred names.

items.title: Key Takeaways From Our European Healthcare Leaders Conference
items.summary: We provide key takeaways from our European Healthcare Leaders Conference for the big European Pharma names. We hosted management from AstraZeneca, Novartis, Novo Nordisk, Roche and Sanofi, with analysis from those conversations in this note.

items.title: U.S. Regional Banks: Conference Takeaways
items.summary: Our second annual Regional Bank Day saw management teams from SMID banks paint a broadly constructive picture, defined by healthy loan-growth trends, waning deposit-costs capture, resilient credit quality and a renewed focus on capital efficiency. We offer further takeaways in this note.

items.title: AI at the Edge: Verification, Agents and Infrastructure
items.summary: Two recent events gave us a map of applied AI's frontier: one on the infrastructure and deployment layer, the other on the trust and reliability problem that becomes more urgent the more AI gets deployed. The industry is now wrestling with harder, less glamorous problems, as explored here.

items.title: Retail Weekend Reading: Revisiting the First Half's Hot Topics
items.summary: As has become our summer tradition, we use this week's Retail Weekend Reading to take a look back at topics we discussed in our notes from the first half of the year. We include links to the original notes, summaries, and updates to key charts, data and views.

items.title: European Rates Weekly: When, Not If?
items.summary: The next ECB hike seems more a question of when — July or September — than if. As far as the ECB’s balance-sheet outlook is concerned, we argue that the backdrop for short-dated repo rates should remain benign, but further excess liquidity distribution will get harder from here.

items.title: European Equity Road Ahead: Resilient Earnings, Rising Horizons
items.summary: We remain constructive on European equities to mid-2027. While macro headwinds are building, positioning remains light, the case for owning global equities is still strong, and tailwinds from fiscal policy should support diversification into European equities over the longer-term.

items.title: Oil Monitor: Amid Rising Tensions, a Look at China Crude Imports
items.summary: As U.S.-Iran tensions surge again, Brent oil prices rose off Tuesday intraday lows. Meanwhile, a key issue for oil markets is China’s crude oil imports, which have fallen from an elevated 12.6 million barrels a day in February to 7.8 million in May. We offer further analysis in this note.

items.title: Mexico Economics Weekly: An Activity Rebound ... With Caveats
items.summary: We expect a solid rebound in second-quarter GDP, though data point to a fragile recovery for now, with momentum staying soft and demand indicators weak. Looking ahead, uncertainty around the USMCA review remains a key downside risk to the recovery in the second half of the year.

items.title: Emerging Markets Strategy Weekly: Watching U.S. Real Yields
items.summary: Our recent global EM conference gave us feedback on how clients see market risk. Many clients are now focusing — correctly — on the recent rise in U.S. real yields and what that means for risky assets. As we note herein, higher real rates may not be that detrimental for EMFX.

items.title: Will the ECB Raise Rates Again in July?
items.summary: The ECB raised rates 25 basis points; we see little in its communications to make us think another such hike is unlikely in July. Such a move seems like the only course of action consistent with talk that inflation must be brought down and talk of monetary policy's role in achieving that.

items.title: Artificial Intelligence: Rationing Comes for Us All
items.summary: The health of the AI trade will remain defined by the irresistible force of enterprise demand meeting the immovable object of inference capacity, where the reaction continues to come in higher costs, causing enterprises to ration AI spending. We explore the impacts of this dynamic herein.

items.title: GLP-1 Survey: A Headwind for Brands That Choose Not to Evolve
items.summary: Our U.S. survey of people pursuing weight management methods suggests restaurants are near the bullseye of behavioral changes that could pose an $18 billion headwind to industry sales by 2030. Fast food appears the most vulnerable, with distributors the best insulated.

items.title: Muscle Memory, But How Cheap Is Ferrari on Slower Growth?
items.summary: Amid the noise around the new Luce BEV launch, Ferrari shares have actually been trading well, with an increasingly clear base forming in the €280-€310 range, offering support for the first time in 12 months. But we think a number of risks remain for Neutral-rated RACE, as explored herein.

items.title: Argentina's Tightrope: Reform, Trust and a Race Against the Clock
items.summary: Argentina's reform process is advancing on solid macro foundations but faces headwinds from weakening domestic demand and eroding public confidence. Investor trust remains the most critical asset, as the costs of reform are immediate while the benefits are delayed. We discuss risks herein.

items.title: Initial Jobless Claims Are Trending Higher
items.summary: Initial jobless claims increased to a higher-than-expected 229,000 and seem to be picking up on a four-week moving average into the summer months, as has been the case in recent years. If it continues, this trend would point to weakness across other labor-market data into the summer.

items.title: World Radar Screen: Gains for Value-Momentum
items.summary: Value–Momentum strategies delivered robust performance across most regions in May, with particularly strong returns in Asia and Emerging Markets. In contrast, returns lagged with weaker outcomes in the U.S. and the UK. We offer further analysis in this note.

items.title: Quantitative Global Macro Strategy: Regime Model Update
items.summary: We update our regime model using the latest country-level data. Cross-asset volatility has declined further since last month, and the longer-term impact of the oil supply shock data (and duration) remains uncertain. The model flags analogous periods in 1976–77 and down-weights 1996–97.

items.title: Global Theme Machine: AI and Industrial Themes Extend Their Lead
items.summary: The Theme Model showed significant dispersion, with top-performing themes generating returns in excess of 10%, while laggards declined between 1% and 2%. Top-performing themes were Greening the Home, Cloud Computing, Wearable Technology, AI Enablers, and Manufacturing Onshoring.

items.title: Correlation Corner: Expressions for U.S. Exceptionalism
items.summary: A sharp divergence in economic data, with accelerating U.S. momentum and a weakening G9. likely signals a return to U.S. exceptionalism. This regime would likely support more medium-term U.S. equity strength, despite the triggering of recent tactical risk-off signals, and a stronger USD.

items.title: The High-Grade Beat: The AI Credit Cycle Broadens
items.summary: AI's influence on U.S. investment-grade credit continues to broaden, extending beyond hyperscaler supply into project finance structures and rating action. The first round of IG credit rating actions has gone to the bulls, resolving some of the uncertainty over which credits AI helps.

items.title: China Economics: PPI Reflation Is More Than Just an Energy Shock
items.summary: China's inflation readings came in mixed for May. We stick with our forecast for CPI and PPI at 1.0% and 2.8% year over year, respectively, and see labor market conditions as a trigger for more policy adjustments. We explore the details and offer further analysis in this note.

items.title: China Economics: The "Six Networks" Arrive
items.summary: China is accelerating policy implementation to stabilize investment, with the recent launch of the Three Gorges project, a significant AI buildout plan for data centers, and a renewed push for urban renewal. All three fill in details of the “Six Networks” initiative, as we explore herein.

items.title: Macro Trends May Raise Risks for U.S. Advertising
items.summary: U.S. advertising trends have been healthy, with TV stations the only segment to hint at potential weakness. But two macro indicators — a low savings rate and rising gas prices — suggest U.S. ad spending may face some pressures in the second half of this year or in 2027.

items.title: Our Global Athletic Survey Looks Slightly More Positive for Nike
items.summary: Our quarterly global athletic survey points to a weaker macro backdrop across all regions, though promotional levels seemed to have improved this quarter in North America and China, with Europe unchanged. Nike gained or held vs. Adidas across regions; we offer other brand takeaways herein.

items.title: European Auto Manufacturers: The Other Side?
items.summary: European auto makers have been suffering from a combination of severe structural thematic headwinds, sharp EPS downgrades and a complete lack of positive equity narrative in a world increasingly driven by momentum. We look at two ways this underperformance could change and what to watch.

items.title: Global FX Strategy: Our Risk Sentiment Goes From Yellow to Red
items.summary: In the last week our risk sentiment has moved from green to yellow and now to red. While we still see a positive trend for risk assets, multiple catalysts at play would likely lead to risk-off with higher yields; historically, that's a USD-positive backdrop. We consider strategies herein.

items.title: Will the Tech-Led Correction in Japanese Equities Drag On?
items.summary: Japanese high-tech stocks had been driving the market since April but have now seen a significant correction, resulting in sharp declines in both the Nikkei and TOPIX. But we see four reasons to think this tech-led correction will likely be short-lived, with significant upside still ahead.

items.title: Metal Matters: Why This May Not Be the Gold Dip to Buy
items.summary: Gold recently closed below its 200dMA for the first time since September 2023. We lower our 0-3-month point-price target to $4,000 an ounce from $4,300. While we maintain a bullish longer-term view of gold, we explain why this may not be the dip to buy.

items.title: Metal Matters: Why We Remain Bullish Lithium
items.summary: Recent lithium price declines likely reflect increased GFEX stockpiles, improved supply responsiveness, and continued uncertainty around near-term end use demand. But we think the majority of the sell-off is behind us, with prices likely to consolidate in the near term.

items.title: Deep Dive: Unpacking Baidu's AI Cloud Potential
items.summary: Baidu's sustained commitment to AI has produced formidable, vertically integrated full-stack capabilities; we see a pivotal shift that we think is transforming it from a legacy search-dominated business to an AI-centric company. We look at potential avenues for monetization we see ahead.

items.title: Sizing the BoursoBank Opportunity for Societe Generale
items.summary: We expect BoursoBank to be a pillar of Societe Generale’s September investor day. After rapid customer growth in recent years, the next step is to enhance monetization. Looking ahead, we expect greater emphasis on customer service and products to keep growing the customer base.

items.title: Are UK Life Insurers Becoming Global Acquisition Targets?
items.summary: Over the past year, four long-term capital allocators have either finalized or secured long-term partnerships with UK Life insurers. We address key questions about the increasing exposure of AAM in this market, the potential impact on the competitive landscape, and valuation implications.

items.title: Key Takeaways From Our India Investor Conference 2026
items.summary: Our Annual Investor Conference in Mumbai gathered more than 100 companies for track sessions on topics including India's outlook and growth story, AI, geopolitics and more. We offer key takeaways from the conference and thoughts on sector impacts in this note.

items.title: Mongolia Economics: Diversification Gains Face Energy Test
items.summary: Following meetings with officials and analysts in Ulaanbaatar, we remain constructive on Mongolia's near-term macro prospects, forecasting above-consensus 7% GDP growth for 2026. The next developmental priority is to ease strained energy infrastructure, as discussed herein.

items.title: Africa Weekly: Algeria, Angola and Currency Stability
items.summary: Central banks in both Algeria and Angola will probably be able to maintain stability for the dinar and kwanza this year and into 2027, helped in part by oil prices' rise. But the ability to keep both currencies stable over the longer term remains uncertain.
